emails.body:
Good morning,

Senator Lane has expressed interest in receiving a classified briefing on the ATaS program's current status and near-term roadmap. The Senator is a strong supporter of modernization efforts but has made clear that rigorous oversight accompanies that support.

We are targeting the week of 7 October for a 90-minute session in the Capitol Visitor Center SVC-217. Please confirm availability for COL Abreu and LCDR Maddox to present, along with any clearance or access requirements on your end.

Respectfully,
Capt Alicia Scott
Congressional Liaison Office
Joint Interagency Task Force

Capt Scott,

Thank you for the request. The Senator's schedule is tight that week, but we can hold Monday, 7 October at 0900 for 90 minutes in SVC-217. Please provide the attendee list and bios by COB 3 October so we can process building access.

We'll need the briefing deck at least 48 hours in advance for staff review. The Senator will likely focus on validation metrics, transition risks, and cost trajectory.

Best,
Senator Lane Staff
Senate Armed Services Committee

Capt Scott,

Confirmed — COL Abreu and I are available Monday, 7 October at 0900. Regarding Pentagon access for any staff requiring escort: the JIAITF visitor control center at the River Entrance (Gate 4) opens at 0600. Badging typically takes 15–20 minutes if pre-cleared. Please send full names, organizations, and citizenship for each attendee by Wednesday so we can submit the access request.

We'll plan to arrive by 0815 for a quick sync before the brief.

V/r,
LCDR James Maddox
Deputy Director, ATaS
Joint Interagency Task Force

Capt Scott, Senator Lane Staff, LCDR Maddox —

The 0900 start on Monday works. I'll be traveling from Norfolk that morning; wheels down at DCA by 0715, so I'll meet you at the River Entrance around 0800.

One note: the Senator's staff mentioned focus areas of validation metrics, transition risks, and cost trajectory. I've asked the team to pull the latest test reports and the updated POA&M. We'll have talking points aligned to those lines of effort by COB Friday.

See you Monday.

COL Jesse Abreu
Director, ATaS
Joint Interagency Task Force

Senator Lane Staff,

Attached is the calendar invite with the finalized attendee list and bios for Monday's briefing. Attendees from JIAITF:

- COL Jesse Abreu, Director, ATaS
- LCDR James Maddox, Deputy Director, ATaS
- Ms. Karen Velez, Chief Engineer, ATaS (available for technical Q&A)
- Mr. David Chen, Program Analyst, ATaS (cost/transition)

All four hold active TS/SCI clearances. Citizenship and organization details are in the invite notes.

We will deliver the briefing deck by COB Saturday, 5 October, for your 48-hour review.

Respectfully,
Capt Alicia Scott
Congressional Liaison Office
Joint Interagency Task Force

Capt Scott,

Received. The attendee list is approved. Building access requests have been submitted for all four names.

A few additional items from the Senator's office:
1. Please ensure the deck includes a one-page executive summary up front.
2. The Senator wants to see the independent validation timeline — not just internal test results.
3. Cost slide should break out FY26 execution vs. FY27 PB request.

We'll have staff comments back to you by Sunday evening at the latest.

Thanks,
Senator Lane Staff
Senate Armed Services Committee

Capt Scott, Senator Lane Staff, COL Abreu —

Confirming Monday, 7 October at 0900 in SVC-217. We'll be there by 0815 as planned.

Deck is on track for Saturday delivery. The team is incorporating the three items you flagged: executive summary, independent validation timeline, and the FY26/FY27 cost breakout. Validation metrics are tracking to the 1% threshold we discussed; the final test report closes out this week.

See you Monday.

V/r,
LCDR James Maddox
Deputy Director, ATaS
Joint Interagency Task Force

Capt Scott,

Final logistics for Monday:
- Meet at River Entrance (Gate 4) by 0800. I'll have escorts for the team.
- Parking authorized in Lot 12 (overflow) if needed.
- Briefing deck v13 uploaded to the shared drive; hard copies will be in the room.
- Talking points aligned to the three focus areas: validation, transition, cost.

Calendar invite re-attached with room details and POCs. Safe travel for those coming in Sunday.

COL Jesse Abreu
Director, ATaS
Joint Interagency Task Force
